rulings.Case Title: JEFFERSON CAPITAL SYSTEMS, LLC VS. KIMSHREE S MAUFAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Moiton To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar in Dept. 302 for Monday, Mar-03-2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF JEFFERSON CAPITAL SYSTEMS, LLC Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment. The matter is ordered off calendar due to the moving party's failure to comply with SFLR 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC. ET AL VS. NKECHI EMERUWA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Facts Admitted And For A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Her Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar in Dept. 302 for Monday, Mar-03-2025, Line 5. 1-PLAINTIFF BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC.'S Motion To Deem Facts Admitted And For A Monetary Sanction Against Defendant And Her Counsel Of Record. The only issue before the Court is sanctions. 1. If defense counsel had properly verified the discovery requests, counsel would not have started down this bumpy road. 2. Nor could defense counsel unilaterally give herself an extension to provide the necessary verifications, particularly since they were already past due. 3. Accordingly, Plaintiff is entitled to $1060.00 as reasonable sanctions payable by defendant and her attorney of record, jointly and severally. Sanctions are to be paid within 10 days of service of order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: JORGE VASQUEZ VALDEZ VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar in Dept. 302 for Monday, Mar-03-2025, Line 11. PLAINTIFF JORGE VALDEZ', AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Motion to compel/Denied. 1.First, the Court notes Plaintiff's counsel did not respond to the issue of timeliness. 2. Defense counsel is correct . Motion is in violation of CCP Sec. 2030.300. Plaintiff had 45 days from September 13 to file motion. that date was October 28 not the 30th as argued by defendant. Regardless the motion was 8 days late. 3.See Sexton v. Superior Court (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1410 [court lacks authority to rule on an untimely motion]; Vidal Sassoon, Inc. v. Superior Court (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 681, 685 [time limitations to bring a motion to compel are mandatory]. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: BLANE R. PRESCOTT ET AL VS. 355 BRYANT STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar in Dept. 302 for Monday, Mar-03-2025, Line 9. DEFENDANT 355 BRYANT STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Further Responses To Discovery. Motion to compel further responses to its (1) form interrogatories (set one); (2) special interrogatories (set one); and (3) request for production (set one)/ Granted. 1. Defendants have not elevated form over substance. The cited interrogatories and requests for production are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) 2. Defendants have shown they met and conferred in a reasonable and good faith manner and Plaintiffs' responses are incomplete, evasive, and/or composed of meritless objections. (Mayer Decl., pars. 7-11.) 3. In response, Plaintiffs promised to provide amended responses by January 10, 2025, but failed to do so. (Mayer Decl., par. 10, Ex. G.) 4. Defendants' form interrogatories properly seek general background information. Furthermore, for the few interrogatories for which Plaintiffs filed substantive responses, even those were incomplete. (See, e.g., Form Interrogatory 2.5 [plaintiffs failed to provide dates].) 5. Plaintiff must provide verified responses to the discovery requests. For the document responses to which Plaintiffs claim a privilege, Plaintiffs shall produce a privilege log. Further responses, verification and privilege log are due no later than 10 court days from service of this order. 6. Finally, the Court finds Plaintiffs acted without substantial justification and sanctions are imposed on Plaintiffs' counsel in the amount of $3,410. Said sanctions are to be paid within 10 days of service of the order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: RED OAK REALTY VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar in Dept. 302 for Monday, Mar-03-2025, Line 4. DEFENDANTS VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., JAMES NUNEMACHER, FRANK NOLAN, and PAMELA HOFFMAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order. Motion for protective order/Granted. 1. CCP 2019.210 provides: "[i]n any action alleging the misappropriation of a trade secret under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act, before commencing discovery relating to the trade secret, the party alleging the misappropriation shall identify the trade secret with reasonable particularity" 2. In Advanced Modular Sputtering, Inc. v. Superior Court (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 826, 835, the court explained that where "every cause of action is factually dependent on the misappropriation allegation, discovery can commence only after the allegedly misappropriated trade secrets have been identified with reasonable particularity." 3. In the instant case, the breach of contract claim is based on the misuse of trade secrets/confidential information. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC") pars. 33, 34, 38.) The tortious interference and slander causes of action are similarly predicated on the misuse of the alleged trade secrets. (FAC, 25, 41, 48-50, 53.) 4. The Court agrees Plaintiff violated CCP 2019.210 and acted without substantial justification by prematurely serving the deposition notices, which are quashed. 5. Defendants are entitled to $4,772 as reasonable sanctions payable by Plaintiff Red Oak Realty and its counsel of record Richard T. Bowles and Colin G. Furlow of Bowles & Verna LLP, jointly and severally. Sanctions are to be paid within 10 days of service of order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)
